data.title: Market Chatter: Japanese Corporate Profits Expected to Decline in Fiscal 2026
data.description:
Total net profits for listed Japanese companies are projected to decline for the first time in six years, falling 7% in the fiscal year ending March 2026, according to data compiled by Nikkei Asia Review.

This downturn is primarily attributed to trade and currency headwinds impacting manufacturers, especially those reliant on the U.S. market, said the news daily.

The auto sector faces a significant 32% drop, while steelmakers are bracing for a 27% profit decline, added the news agency.

data.title: Woojin I&S Wins 10.8 Billion Won Order
data.description:
Woojin I&S Co Ltd :

data.title: South Korea Producer Prices Rise 0.9% in April
data.description:
Producer prices in South Korea increased 0.9% in April from a year earlier, according to preliminary data from the Bank of Korea on Friday.

The on-year gains were primarily driven by higher costs across electric power, gas, water and waste, and services, the preliminary data indicated.

The prices for agricultural foods, livestock products, and marine products declined by 1.3%.

On a month-on-month basis, producer prices fell 0.1% in April, according to the data.

data.title: China Injects 500 Billion Yuan to Boost Liquidity
data.description:
The People's Bank of China is boosting liquidity by conducting a 500-billion-yuan one-year medium-term lending facility, Xinhua News reported Thursday.

The central bank will use fixed-quantity, interest-rate-bidding methods for the Friday operation.

This follows China's pledge for a moderately loose monetary policy in 2024, with the PBOC deploying multiple tools to stabilize rates.

data.title: Samsung Electronics Introduces Galaxy S25 Edge in South Korea
data.description:
Samsung Electronics released the Galaxy S25 Edge in South Korea on Friday, the South Korean electronics products maker said in a same-day release.

The device features a sleek design, enhanced durability, and the Snapdragon 8 Elite for Galaxy chipset. The product, available in titanium silver, jet black, and ice blue, is priced at 1,496,000 won for the 256GB model and 1,639,000 won for the 512GB version, the release said.

data.title: Hengrui Pharma's Hong Kong Listing Nets HK$9.8 Billion
data.description:
Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals , priced its initial public offering in Hong Kong at the upper limit of its indicative range to raise HK$9.75 billion in net proceeds.

The Chinese drugmaker issued 224.5 million shares at HK$44.05 each, the top end of its guidance of as low as HK$41.45 per share, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong Exchange.

The deal's Hong Kong public offering portion was 454.9 times subscribed, prompting the company to issue 48.3 million shares, a near fourfold increase from the 12.3 million shares initially allotted.

The international offering portion was 17.1 times subscribed, and the firm issued 176.2 million shares, lower than the initial allocation of 212.2 million.

The cornerstone investors, including Singaporean sovereign wealth fund GIC, subscribed to a total of 93.7 million IPO shares.

Hengrui Pharma starts trading on the Hong Kong bourse today, May 23.

data.title: Press Metal Aluminium Outlook Likely Steady Despite Tariff Headwinds — Market Talk
data.description: Press Metal Aluminium's long-term outlook may remain supported by tight aluminum supply despite headwinds from U.S. tariffs, say Maybank IB analysts Loh Yan Jin and Jeremie Yap in a note. The company's upstream and downstream expansions are expected to support margin stability and mitigate supply chain risks, they say. However, the analysts cut Press Metal's 2025-2027 net profit forecasts by 3%, 5%, and 5%, respectively, to reflect lower aluminum price assumptions and reduced associate contributions. Maybank lowers Press Metal's target price to MYR5.64 from MYR5.82 after 1Q earnings came in slightly below expectations, but maintains a buy rating on favorable supply dynamics. Shares are up 0.4% at MYR5.05. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: PENN Entertainment Executives Increase Their Holdings
data.description:
Reporter NameRelationshipTypeAmountSEC Filing
Snowden Jay ACEO and PresidentPurchase$499,766Form 4
Handler David ADirectorPurchase$148,264Form 4

Jay A. Snowden, CEO and President of PENN Entertainment, as well as a Director, has expanded his stake in the company by purchasing 34,000 shares of Common Stock on May 22, 2025. The shares were acquired at a weighted average price of $14.699, amounting to a total investment of $499,766. This transaction increases Snowden's direct ownership to 1,082,625 shares, with the purchase price range between $14.66 and $14.8149.

David A Handler, a Director at PENN Entertainment, also made a significant investment by buying 10,000 shares of Common Stock on the same day, at a price of $14.8264 per share, totaling $148,264. Following this acquisition, Handler's direct ownership has risen to 322,941 shares, in addition to indirectly owning 20,000 shares through a foundation.

data.title: Asia must not succumb to tariff retaliation, Singapore cbank official says
data.description:
Asian economies must remain agile and not succumb to tit-for-tat tariff retaliation, a deputy managing director of the Monetary Authority of Singapore said on Friday.

Retaliatory tariffs would lead to negative supply shifts that would worsen the growth-inflation trade-off and complicate monetary policy, Edward Robinson, who is also the MAS's chief economist, told a monetary policy conference.

"They should continue to keep the old advice to avoid throwing rocks into their own harvest and intensify regional trade integration initiatives, including in digital and services trade, and investment," Robinson said.

Protectionism and import taxes disrupt resource allocation and lower the consumer surplus as domestic households face higher prices and fewer choices, he said.

"Both the targeted and the tariff-imposing economies suffer."

Despite having a free-trade agreement and running a trade deficit with the United States, Singapore has been slapped with a 10% baseline tariff rate by Washington.

Other Southeast Asian countries have been threatened with much higher tariffs, although they have been delayed until July and an interim 10% tariff is in place for now.

Singapore on Thursday reported a 0.6% contraction in the first quarter, even before U.S. tariffs were announced, putting the economy at risk of a technical recession.

The MAS eased policy at reviews in January and April this year. Speaking on Thursday after the GDP data, Robinson said the current monetary policy stance remained appropriate.

data.title: Some US banks explore venturing into crypto with joint stablecoin, WSJ reports
data.description:
Some of the biggest U.S. banks are exploring whether to team up to issue a joint stablecoin, The Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday.

The conversations have so far involved companies co-owned by JPMorgan Chase , Bank of America , Citigroup , Wells Fargo and other large commercial banks, the report said, citing people familiar with the matter.

However, the newspaper said that the bank consortium discussions are in early, conceptual stages and could change.

Reuters could not immediately confirm the report. The banks did not respond to Reuters' requests for comment late on Thursday.

Stablecoins, a type of cryptocurrency designed to maintain a constant value, usually pegged to a fiat currency such as U.S. dollar, are commonly used by crypto traders to move funds between tokens.

One bank consortium possibility that has been discussed would be a model that lets other banks use the stablecoin, in addition to the co-owners of the Clearing House and Early Warning Services, the Journal said, citing unnamed sources.

Some regional and community banks have also considered whether to pursue a separate stablecoin consortium, it added.

Trump has promised to be the "crypto president," popularizing its mainstream use in the U.S. He has said he backs crypto because it can improve the banking system and increase the dominance of the dollar.

data.title: Container Corporation of India Gets Board Nod for Issue of Bonus Shares
data.description:
Container Corporation of India received board approval for the issuance of bonus equity shares, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The company plans to issue one equity share of 5 rupees each for every four fully paid-up equity shares of 5 rupees each held by eligible shareholders.

The bonus shares of 761.6 million Indian rupees will be issued from and out of retained earnings as available on March 31.

These bonus shares are scheduled to be issued by July 21.

data.title: Japan's Core-Core Inflation May Drop to Near 1.5% in Early 2026 — Market Talk
data.description: Increases in Japan's consumer price index, excluding volatile fresh food and energy prices, is likely to slow to somewhere between 1.5% and 2.0% in 1Q 2026, says Mizuho Securities economist Ryosuke Katagi. The so-called core-core CPI rose 3.0% on year in April, vs March's 2.9% rise. The measure is considered a gauge of underlying inflation. There appears to be no signs of acceleration in service prices despite wage growth, while cost-push factors including a spike in rice prices are expected to ease, Katagi says. "From the perspective of stably achieving 2% inflation, it still lacks strength," he says. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

data.title: Market Chatter: Mitsui O.S.K. Lines Assessing Impact of EU Sanctions on LNG Vessels
data.description:
Mitsui O.S.K. Lines , Japan's second-largest shipping company, is evaluating the impact of new European Union sanctions against Russia on three of its liquefied natural gas vessels, Reuters reported Thursday, citing a company spokesperson.

The company said it will fully cooperate with relevant authorities, including the EU and Japanese government, and remains committed to complying with all applicable regulations, the newswire said.

The EU's 17th package of sanctions, targeting Moscow's shadow fleet, specifically included MOL's North Moon, North Ocean, and North Light LNG vessels, the report added.

data.title: Uranium Firms' Shares Soar After Reports of US Plans to Boost Nuclear Energy Industry
data.description:
The stock prices of Australian uranium firms soared in recent trading on Friday, after Reuters reported Friday that US President Donald Trump plans to sign an executive order to ease the regulatory process on approvals for new nuclear reactors and strengthen their fuel supply chains, citing four sources.

Shares of Boss Energy surged 13%, while those of Deep Yellow jumped 9%. Paladin Energy's stock price advanced over 9%.

data.title: Sunway's Sales Likely to Rise in Coming Quarters — Market Talk
data.description: Sunway Bhd.'s sales are expected to pick up in the coming quarters, Maybank IB analyst Wong Wei Sum writes in a note. There are MYR3.7 billion worth of new launches planned for the conglomerate from May, Wong says. During 1Q, Sunway secured MYR555 million in property sales, representing 15% of its 2025 property sales target of MYR3.6 billion, Wong notes. Maybank likes Sunway for its diversified income base and hands-on management. It raises Sunway's target price to MYR5.14 from MYR5.02, but maintains a hold rating as it sees most positives as priced in. Shares are 0.2% lower at MYR4.82.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: TSMC warns US chip tariffs could undermine its Arizona investment
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) has warned that proposed U.S. tariffs on foreign-made chips could jeopardize its massive US$165 billion investment in advanced semiconductor manufacturing facilities in Arizona. In a public comment submitted to the U.S. Commerce Department, the company cautioned that reduced demand resulting from the tariffs could impact the viability of its U.S. operations, according to a report by PC Mag.

TSMC highlighted that its Arizona fabs will eventually account for about 30% of the company’s global capacity for cutting-edge 2nm and more advanced chips—enough to meet projected U.S. demand. The chipmaker also confirmed it has begun construction on a third fabrication plant in the state, which will initially produce 2nm chips and later adopt its next-generation A16 process technology.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) is the world’s largest contract chipmaker and a key player in the global semiconductor supply chain. It produces advanced chips for major tech firms like Apple, NVIDIA, and AMD, and is known for leading-edge manufacturing technologies such as 3nm and 2nm process nodes.

data.title: The USD is losing some ground in Asian trade
data.description:
The USD is slipping vs. other major FX in Asia trade.

Apart from what has been posted there isn't any fresh news.

EUR, AUD, CAD, GBP, NZD all higher. USD/JPY and USD/CHF are losing a few tics also.

data.title: Mirxes Shares Jump 24% on Hong Kong Debut
data.description:
Mirxes made an impressive trading debut in Hong Kong on Friday morning as its shares soared over 24% at the opening bell.

The Singapore-headquartered micro ribonucleic acid technology company opened at HK$29 per share, well above its IPO price of HK$23.30.

data.title: MTAR Technologies Plans Merger with Two Wholly Owned Arms to Streamline Operations
data.description:
MTAR Technologies approved a proposal to merge with its two wholly owned subsidiaries, Gee Pee Aerospace and Defence and Magnatar Aerosystems, according to a Thursday filing on the local bourses.

The merger is intended to simplify the group's structure, reduce administrative costs, and improve financial consolidation. No new shares will be issued under the proposed scheme.

data.title: Dhunseri Tea to Sell Deohall Tea Estate
data.description:
Dhunseri Tea & Industries agreed to sell its Deohall tea estate to Madhuting Tea for 235 million Indian rupees, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The transaction is expected to be completed by June 30.

data.title: Meituan's First-Quarter Profit Likely Jumped on Stronger Revenue — Earnings Preview
data.description:
By Tracy Qu

Meituan is scheduled to report results for the first quarter on Monday. Here is what you need to know:

NET PROFIT: The Chinese food-delivery company is expected to report net profit of 7.93 billion yuan, equivalent to $1.10 billion, according to the consensus estimate of nine analysts polled by FactSet. That would be sharply higher than the 5.37 billion yuan reported in the year-earlier period.

REVENUE: Revenue is estimated at 85.4 billion yuan, according to FactSet, up from 73.275 billion yuan a year ago.

The company's Hong Kong-listed shares rose 2.7% in the first three months of the year, underperforming the benchmark Hang Seng Index's 15% gain that came amid optimism about Chinese AI startup DeepSeek and Beijing's more favorable stance toward private enterprises. Since then, Meituan's stock has fallen about 13% as macroeconomic uncertainties weighed on investors' sentiment globally.

WHAT TO WATCH:

  • DOMESTIC COMPETITION: Investors will be interested in whether stiff competition from other Chinese food-delivery players has hindered Meituan's revenue growth. Citi analysts said in a research note that the initial food-delivery volume achieved by e-commerce giant JD.com, a new entrant, is expected to have a limited impact on Meituan's volume in the near term. However, they said the increased competition will likely force Meituan to step up platform subsidies for users, riders and merchants and accelerate the rollout of rider benefits, which will weigh on revenue growth and unit economics, an indicator measuring profitability per order in the delivery industry.
  • OVERSEAS EXPANSION: Meituan is expected to provide an update on its overseas-expansion initiatives after the results, Citi analysts wrote in a separate note. While Brazil could be the company's next focus, it might also expand its Keeta app to other Arab nations in 2025, they said. The analysts noted that Meituan's international food-delivery app could enjoy a high user penetration, frequency and average order value in these new markets.

Write to Tracy Qu at tracy.qu@wsj.com
